mission.title: linkerd: Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque
mission.description: Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current linkerd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your linkerd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
helm list -n linkerd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working linkerd installation.

steps.title: Review linkerd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant linkerd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get configmap -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=linkerd
```
### What is the issue?

If a HTTP is sent to a port which is marked as opaque, the proxy returns a 404 response with `no route found for request` as the error, even if no Server resource is defined for that port.

### How can it be reproduced?

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque
steps.description:
One aspect of this that jumps out at me is the subtle difference between what we call "opaque protocol" and "opaque transport".

Opaque protocol is enabled by setting the opaque ports annotation on the Service and instructs the outbound proxy to skip protocol detection.  Opaque transport is enabled
```yaml
# curl -v web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local
[...]
*   Trying 10.96.92.112...
* TCP_NODELAY set
* Expire in 200 ms for 4 (transfer 0x55fded8edf50)
* Connected to web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local (10.96.92.112) port 80 (#0)
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: web-svc.emojivoto.svc.cluster.local
> User-Agent: curl/7.64.0
> Accept: */*
> 
< HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
< l5d-proxy-error: no route found for request
< date: Thu, 17 Nov 2022 22:53:06 GMT
< content-length: 0
<
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=linkerd
kubectl get events -n linkerd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Proxy returns 404 when HTTP connection is marked as opaque" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
One aspect of this that jumps out at me is the subtle difference between what we call "opaque protocol" and "opaque transport".

Opaque protocol is enabled by setting the opaque ports annotation on the Service and instructs the outbound proxy to skip protocol detection.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with linkerd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
